title: Qwen3-VL-8B INT4 — CPU visual-design-critique latency (VLM design loop, UC-010 Phase 3)
grade: proof / single-run (NOT a rigorous multi-run benchmark)

hardware.gpu_present: Intel Arc 140V (Xe2) — NOT used (run on CPU to avoid GPU/OVMS contention)

methodology.task: shared.fleet.critique.critique_screenshot over a real flawed-app screenshot (1280x735 PNG)

not_measured: GPU latency (in a real dispatch the VLM co-resides with the 30B [~23 GB < 31.3] and runs on GPU — faster; not measured here)
co-resident VLM + 30B peak (the dispatch case; the 14B is swapped out during a dispatch so it does not co-reside)
result: The REAL critique pipeline ran end-to-end on a real flawed app: the VLM correctly returned VERDICT: NEEDS_WORK and identified the actual design flaws (garish red background, misaligned/cramped buttons, blank display). parse_critique extracted needs_work=True + actionable feedback; should_iterate(iter=0,max=3)=True. The VLM design loop's critique limb is proven on real data.
finding: CPU critique is usable for a standalone proof (~1 min/critique). In a real dispatch the VLM runs on the GPU co-resident with the 30B (the 14B is out), which is faster; the loop unloads the VLM (vlm.unload) after each pass.
